summary: Cipher backend iteration 2 — new endpoints for guest registration/login/upgrade and per-server custom emojis. 18/18 new pytest tests PASSED on the public URL. 13/13 regression tests from iteration 1 also PASSED. No bugs found; all specified edge cases covered (wrong recovery code → 401, non-guest guest-login → 401, non-guest upgrade → 400, duplicate email on upgrade → 400, permanent login after upgrade works, emoji duplicate name → 400, >512KB emoji → 400, non-member upload → 404, non-uploader non-owner delete → 403, /api/emojis strictly filters by membership, server_name attached, /emojis/{id}/image returns exact bytes).

minor.issue: Returns HTTP 200 while the review spec expected 201. Minor semantics; clients relying on 201 would need status==200 tolerance. Suggest explicit status_code=201 in decorator.

Uses `name` as a bare function parameter → FastAPI treats it as a required query param. Works, but it's more idiomatic to declare `name: str = Query(...)` or accept it as a form field (multipart). Currently undocumented in /docs as a form field.
No MIME validation — a user can upload any file with content_type=image/png. Consider validating magic bytes or allowlisting image/png, image/gif, image/webp.
Guest `recovery_code` has no min-length constraint server-side (Pydantic `str`). If client sends empty string, bcrypt will happily hash it. Add min_length=12 validation.
No password strength validation (min length, complexity). Register uses password min on client; upgrade accepts any string. Minor.
No brute-force lockout on recovery_code attempts; same concern as /auth/login from iteration 1. Since recovery_code is 16 alphanumeric chars entropy is high, but still recommended.
Sync file iterator (yield from f) inside async handler — same pattern as /files/{id}, can block event loop for larger emojis.

Uses `path.unlink(missing_ok=True)` inside try/except — fine, but the underlying UPLOAD_DIR is shared with user file uploads (same uuid space). Low collision risk but worth documenting a separate EMOJI_DIR.

action_items: OPTIONAL: add status_code=201 to the emoji upload decorator to match REST semantics.
OPTIONAL: declare `name` as Form(...) or Query(...) explicitly on /servers/{id}/emojis for clearer OpenAPI.
OPTIONAL: validate emoji upload MIME / magic bytes (image/png|gif|webp only).
OPTIONAL: add min_length on GuestRegisterIn.recovery_code and UpgradeAccountIn.password.
critical_code_review_comments: Guest flow is correctly orthogonal: is_guest=true set on register, login requires is_guest=true filter (prevents bypass), upgrade flips is_guest=false and accepts new encrypted_private_key re-wrapped under the new password — E2EE continuity preserved.
public_user() correctly omits password_hash and encrypted_private_key from responses.
Emoji membership check uses `{members: user_id}` filter — 404 returned for non-members (consistent with other server endpoints to avoid leaking existence).
/api/emojis scoping is implemented via two queries (servers → ids → emojis $in). O(N) but fine at current scale; consider a compound index (server_id,created_at) on emojis collection.
server.py is now 758 lines — crossed 700-line threshold flagged last iteration. Strongly recommend splitting into routers (auth, guest, servers, emojis, dms, files).
Mongo _id consistently excluded via projection {_id:0} and pop('_id',None) after insert_one — verified.
Emoji filename stored as `<uuid>` in UPLOAD_DIR (no extension) — content-type round-trips via DB record. Correct.
No XSS/escaping concern for :emoji_name: shortcodes since name is lowercased and space-replaced (but `:` and other specials aren't stripped — consider regex [a-z0-9_] only).
test_credentials: Admin: admin@cipher.io / Admin@Cipher2026 (PENDING keys; unused here). Each test creates its own TEST_ users / guests.
seed_data_creation: Tests create their own TEST_ users, guests and TEST_EmojiSrv_* servers. No admin seed modifications.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run: `pytest backend/tests/ -v`. New tests live in /app/backend/tests/test_guest_and_emojis.py. Emoji upload uses query-param name + multipart file. PNG bytes are generated programmatically via _make_png (pure stdlib zlib+struct) — no PIL dependency. Size-limit test crafts a ~600KB PNG with a padded tEXt chunk. If frontend testing is requested next, verify: (a) guest registration screen shows & copies the 16-char recovery_code exactly once, (b) :shortcode: rendering swaps for , (c) upgrade form re-wraps private key with new password before POSTing.
rca of the issue: N/A — no backend bugs found. All 18 new endpoint tests and all 13 regression tests pass against the public URL.
